metadata.summary: FinOps cultural practice for cloud financial accountability via INFORM, OPTIMIZE, OPERATE phases. Concrete triggers, checklists, commands, templates.

content.title: FinOps Framework: Cloud Financial Operations
content.summary: FinOps is a cultural practice that brings financial accountability to cloud spending through cross-functional collaboration between Engineering, Finance, and Business teams. It operates in three phases — INFORM (visibility), OPTIMIZE (efficiency), OPERATE (governance) — and addresses a market where 32% of $723.4B annual cloud spend is wasted.

why.p: Cloud spending hit $723.4B in 2025, yet organizations waste 32% ($200B+) on unused resources, overprovisioned instances, and poor visibility. The average budget overrun is 17%. FinOps adoption is growing 46% YoY, with 70% of enterprises forming dedicated FinOps teams — yet only 23% describe themselves as "highly efficient." The gap is closed by structural practice, not individual effort.

Without FinOps, engineering teams optimize for speed and reliability, finance teams see cloud bills as opaque line items, and product teams have no cost signal for trade-off decisions. FinOps creates the shared vocabulary and cadence that aligns all three.

list.item: Cloud bill above $5k/month and growing 20%+ month-over-month with no per-team visibility.
Multi-team or multi-product engineering org needing showback or chargeback reporting.
Pre-IPO or due-diligence phase requiring credible unit economics ($/customer, $/transaction, $/inference).
Reserved Instance or Savings Plan renewal approaching — need data-driven commitment sizing.
Kubernetes cluster shared across teams where pods are unaccounted.
CFO or board flagged cloud cost as a top-3 P&L concern.
Untagged spend exceeds 20% of bill — allocation impossible without remediation.
Cloud bill below $1k/month — FinOps tooling overhead exceeds savings; eyeball billing weekly instead.
Workloads on flat-fee infrastructure (bare metal, fixed VPS) — no elastic cost to optimize.
Crisis cost-cutting that cannot wait — apply blunt instruments first (turn off non-prod overnight, kill orphan resources), then formalize FinOps.
Strictly regulated workloads where security/compliance trumps cost (dedicated HSMs, isolated tenancy).
Annual cloud spending: $723.4B (2025).
Average waste: 32% of budget.
Budget overrun: 17% average.
FinOps adoption growth: 46% YoY.
Enterprises with FinOps teams: 70%.
Organizations "highly efficient": only 23%.
FOCUS specification (FinOps Open Cost & Usage Specification) v1.1 became the de-facto billing schema in 2025; AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI all export FOCUS-compliant CUR.

section.name: phases
section.p: The FinOps framework operates in three sequential but overlapping phases. Always sequence INFORM → OPTIMIZE → OPERATE; skipping INFORM means optimizing blind.

rule.statement: Phase 1 — INFORM: ingest billing data (CUR/FOCUS export), enforce resource tagging with policy-as-code, build real-time dashboards, ship showback reports per team. Exit gate: 95%+ tag-allocation accuracy and every team lead sees their weekly spend.
rule.rationale: Without visibility, optimization is guesswork. Tagging hygiene is the foundation; without 95%+ tag coverage FinOps reports are estimates dressed as facts.

rule.statement: Phase 2 — OPTIMIZE: rightsize compute (Densify/Compute Optimizer), kill idle resources (CloudCustodian), commit to savings (RIs/SPs at 70-80% coverage), tier storage with lifecycle policies, run non-critical workloads on spot. Exit gate: 20-30% measurable cost reduction within 90 days.
rule.rationale: Optimization without visibility produces waste: teams optimize the wrong resources or over-commit on reservations. Always sequence INFORM before OPTIMIZE.

rule.statement: Phase 3 — OPERATE: codify guardrails (Service Control Policies, OPA), wire anomaly detection (Cost Explorer Anomalies, CloudZero), gate IaC PRs with Infracost, run weekly anomaly review and monthly cost review. Exit gate: zero untagged resources merge to main, anomaly MTTR < 24h.
rule.rationale: One-time optimization degrades over time as teams ship new workloads. Governance automates the feedback loop so cost hygiene is self-sustaining.

how-to.step: Step 0 — Confirm scope. Pull last 90 days of consolidated billing. If monthly run-rate < $5k and growth < 20% MoM, stop and revisit in 6 months. Otherwise continue.

Step 1 — Stand up data plane. Enable FOCUS-compliant Cost & Usage Reports to S3/GCS/Azure storage. Wire to a BigQuery / Athena / Snowflake table partitioned by date. Verify rows for the last 7 days exist.
Step 2 — Define tag taxonomy. Mandate exactly five tags: env, team, service, cost-center, owner. Document allowed values in a `tags.yaml`. Reject all other shapes.
Step 3 — Backfill tags. Generate an untagged-resource report; assign to resource owners with a 14-day deadline. Track coverage daily until ≥95%.
Step 4 — Publish first showback. Build per-team dashboards (cost, top-5 services, trend vs last month). Email weekly. No chargeback yet — trust first.
Step 5 — Run the first optimization sprint. Pick the top-3 spenders; for each: rightsize, terminate idle, evaluate commitment coverage. Target a 10% reduction in week one.
Step 6 — Form the FinOps team. Engineering lead + Finance partner + Product PM + executive sponsor. Schedule weekly 30-min sync, monthly 60-min review, quarterly commitment council.
Step 7 — Codify guardrails. Add Infracost to all IaC PRs. Add OPA policies to deny untagged resources. Add budget alerts at 50/80/100/120% of monthly forecast.
Step 8 — Move to chargeback (only when teams trust the data). Map cost-centers to GL codes, run a parallel month, then flip the switch.
Step 9 — Define unit economics. Pick one north-star metric (cost per active user, cost per 1k API calls, cost per inference). Publish weekly.
Step 10 — Continuous improve. Quarterly: review commitment coverage, retire stale tools, refresh training, update tag taxonomy, audit anomaly MTTR.

checklist.check: FOCUS-format CUR/billing export enabled and landing in object storage daily.
Billing data queryable in a warehouse (Athena/BigQuery/Snowflake).
Tag taxonomy documented (env, team, service, cost-center, owner) with allowed values.
Tag-policy-as-code deployed (AWS Tag Policies, Azure Policy, GCP Org Policy).
Untagged-resource report runs daily and sends to owners.
Tag coverage ≥95% measured by cost (not just resource count).
Per-team dashboard exists and is read by the team lead each week.
Showback email shipped weekly for at least 4 consecutive weeks.
Top-10 cost SKUs identified per team.
Budget alerts wired at 50/80/100/120% with explicit owners.
Rightsizing recommendations pulled (AWS Compute Optimizer / Azure Advisor / GCP Recommender).
Idle-resource scan run (unattached EBS, idle ELBs, stopped-but-billed RDS, orphan IPs).
Non-prod auto-stop schedule deployed (nights + weekends).
Commitment coverage ≥70% on steady-state compute (RI/SP/CUD).
Storage lifecycle policies move cold data to IA/Glacier/Archive after 30/90 days.
Spot/preemptible used for batch, dev, CI, stateless web tier where possible.
Kubernetes: HPA + VPA + Cluster Autoscaler / Karpenter enabled.
Egress fees inspected; cross-region/cross-AZ traffic minimized.
NAT gateway alternatives evaluated (VPC endpoints for S3/DynamoDB).
Database tier optimized (Aurora I/O-Optimized vs Standard, Reserved capacity, read-replica fan-out).
Anomaly detection enabled with Slack/email alerts < 30 min lag.
Anomaly MTTR (mean time to resolve / explain) < 24h.
Infracost (or equivalent) gates every IaC PR with cost diff comment.
OPA / Sentinel / Service Control Policies block untagged or oversize resources.
Monthly FinOps review held; minutes published; actions tracked to closure.
Quarterly commitment optimization session with Finance present.
Unit economics dashboard published (cost per X, $/1k requests).
FinOps maturity self-assessment (Crawl/Walk/Run) reviewed twice a year.
Onboarding doc for new engineers covers tag rules and cost ownership.
Annual tool review — keep, replace, or consolidate.

section.name: team-structure
section.p: FinOps requires cross-functional composition to succeed. A FinOps team without finance representation produces technically-optimal but politically-unacceptable decisions; a team without engineering representation produces recommendations that are infeasible to implement.

rule.statement: Form a cross-functional FinOps team with representatives from Engineering (cloud architects, DevOps), Finance (FP&A, cost accountants), Product (product managers, owners), and executive sponsorship. Assign a FinOps Lead accountable for cross-team coordination.
rule.rationale: Chargeback can break engineering culture if introduced without context. Showback first builds trust; chargeback follows when teams understand the numbers.

list.item: Track and analyze cloud spend across all accounts and projects.
Optimize continuously — not as a one-time project.
Set and enforce cost policies and guardrails.
Report to stakeholders with business-level framing (cost per customer, not cost per EC2).
Drive cultural change: engineers own their cloud costs.
Weekly: review anomalies and quick wins.
Monthly: team cost reviews and budget vs actual.
Quarterly: commitment optimization (RI/SP renewals and adjustments).
Annually: strategy review and tool evaluation.

section.name: key-metrics
section.p: Core KPIs to track at all maturity levels:

list.item: Cost Per Unit of Work — e.g., cost per 100k API calls; target: trending down.
GPU Utilization — active compute time; target: near 100%.
Waste Percentage — unused/idle resources; target: below 10%.
Cost Allocation Accuracy — tagged resources; target: above 95%.
Savings Plan Coverage — committed usage; target: 70-80%.
Effective Savings Rate (ESR) — discount earned vs on-demand list; target: above 25%.
Anomaly MTTD/MTTR — detect under 30 min, explain under 24h.

commands.command: aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=2026-04-01,End=2026-05-01 --granularity MONTHLY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=TAG,Key=team

bq query --use_legacy_sql=false 'SELECT service.description, SUM(cost) AS spend FROM `proj.billing.gcp_billing_export_v1_*` WHERE _PARTITIONDATE >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10'

az costmanagement query --type ActualCost --timeframe MonthToDate --scope "/subscriptions/SUBSCRIPTION_ID" --dataset-aggregation '{"totalCost":{"name":"Cost","function":"Sum"}}' --dataset-grouping name=ResourceGroup type=Dimension
az advisor recommendation list --category Cost --output table

kubectl get pvc -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.phase=="Bound") | select(([.metadata.ownerReferences[]?.kind] | inside(["StatefulSet"])) | not) | "\(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name)"'

example.title: Mandatory tag taxonomy (tags.yaml)
example.code:
required_tags: env: [prod, staging, dev, sandbox] team: [platform, growth, data, ai, sre] service: string # snake_case service name cost-center: regex # ^CC-[0-9]{4}$ owner: email # must be valid corporate email
policy: enforcement: deny-on-create grace_period_days: 7 exempt_namespaces: [kube-system, default] notify_channel: "#finops-alerts"

example.title: OPA Rego — deny untagged resources
GitHub Actions — Infracost PR gate
example.code: name: infracost on: pull_request: paths: ['infra/**', '**/*.tf'] jobs: cost-diff: runs-on: ubuntu-latest permissions: { contents: read, pull-requests: write } steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: { ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }} } - uses: infracost/actions/setup@v3 with: { api-key: ${{ secrets.INFRACOST_API_KEY }} } - run: infracost breakdown --path=infra --format=json --out-file=/tmp/base.json - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - run: infracost diff --path=infra --compare-to=/tmp/base.json --format=json --out-file=/tmp/diff.json - run: infracost comment github --path=/tmp/diff.json --repo=${{ github.repository }} --pull-request=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} --github-token=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} --behavior=update - name: fail on >10% increase run: | delta=$(jq '.diffTotalMonthlyCost | tonumber' /tmp/diff.json) base=$(jq '.pastTotalMonthlyCost | tonumber' /tmp/diff.json) awk -v d="$delta" -v b="$base" 'BEGIN { if (b>0 && d/b > 0.10) exit 1 }'

example.title: Monthly FinOps review agenda (paste into doc)
example.code:
# FinOps Monthly Review — YYYY-MM
## 1. Spend snapshot - Total spend MTD: $___ (vs forecast $___, variance ±__%) - Top 5 services by spend - Top 5 movers (delta vs last month)
## 2. Anomalies - New anomalies this month: count, root cause, status - Outstanding anomalies (>7 days unresolved)
## 3. Allocation - Tag coverage by cost: __% (target 95%) - Untagged spend owners + remediation deadline
## 4. Optimization - Wins shipped (rightsizing, idle kill, lifecycle): $ saved - Backlog: top 3 next bets, owners, due dates
## 5. Commitments - RI/SP coverage: __% (target 70-80%) - Utilization: __% - Renewals/adjustments next 90 days
## 6. Unit economics - Cost per active user: $___ (trend) - Cost per 1k API calls: $___ (trend)
## 7. Action items - [ ] Owner — Action — Due

section.name: prompt-finops-audit

prompt.purpose: LLM-assisted FinOps audit of a cloud account

section.prompt:
You are a FinOps analyst. Audit the cloud account described below and produce a prioritized action plan.
INPUTS: - Last 30 days of FOCUS-format billing (CSV attached) - Tag taxonomy (tags.yaml attached) - Current commitments (RI/SP/CUD list attached)
TASKS: 1. Compute tag coverage by cost. Flag any tag with <95% coverage. 2. Identify top-10 cost SKUs and classify each as: rightsize / idle / commit / lifecycle / OK. 3. Estimate $ savings per recommendation with a confidence score (low/med/high). 4. Detect anomalies (>3 sigma vs 30-day rolling mean) and propose probable causes. 5. Score commitment coverage; recommend buys/sells if outside 70-80% band. 6. Produce a 90-day plan in three buckets: INFORM gaps, OPTIMIZE quick wins, OPERATE guardrails.
OUTPUT: - Markdown report with sections: Executive summary, Findings, Recommendations (table: action, owner_role, est_savings_monthly, confidence), 90-day plan. - Cite the SKU/account/region for every recommendation. - Do NOT invent numbers — if data is missing, list it under "Data gaps".

list.item: AWS: Cost Optimization Hub + Amazon Q — AI-powered recommendations, savings estimates.
Google Cloud: FinOps Hub 2.0 + Gemini Cloud Assist — utilization insights, AI anomaly detection.
Azure: Cost Management + Copilot — budget alerts, recommendations.
Oracle: Carbon Emissions Reporting — GHG Protocol compliant sustainability.
CloudZero — AI cost analytics, unit economics.
Spot.io — spot instance management.
Kubecost / OpenCost — Kubernetes cost monitoring (OpenCost is CNCF graduated).
Apptio Cloudability — multi-cloud FinOps, showback/chargeback.
Densify — ML-powered rightsizing.
ProsperOps — automated commitment management (RIs/SPs).
Vantage — multi-cloud reports and alerts.
Infracost — IaC PR cost gates (OSS).
CloudHealth — governance and optimization.
Cloud Custodian — policy-as-code remediation (OSS).
FinOps Certified Practitioner — foundation certification.
FinOps Certified Professional — advanced certification.
FinOps for AI — specialized certification, launched 2025.
FOCUS Certified — billing-schema specialization, 2026.
FinOps automation standard for 75% of enterprises by 2026.
AI tools managing 80%+ of real-time pricing decisions by 2027.
Predictive modeling reduces overspend by 40% by 2026.
Global savings potential: $100B/year.
FOCUS becomes the universal billing schema; vendor-specific CUR formats deprecate by 2027.

verify.step: Run the INFORM checklist; every box must be ticked. Tag coverage by cost ≥95% verified by a SQL query against the warehouse.
Run the OPTIMIZE checklist; top-3 cost SKUs each have a documented decision (rightsize / commit / leave) with $ impact.
Run the OPERATE checklist; create a synthetic untagged resource via Terraform and confirm the OPA gate blocks the PR.
Confirm Infracost CI job posts a cost-diff comment on a test PR and fails when delta exceeds the threshold.
Trigger a synthetic anomaly (spin up an oversized instance for 2h) and confirm an alert fires within 30 min.
Hold one full monthly review using the template; minutes are stored, action items have owners and due dates.
Compute Effective Savings Rate (ESR) from the latest billing export — value > 25% confirms commitments are productive.
Survey three engineers from different teams: each must name their team's monthly spend within 10% accuracy. If not, INFORM phase failed.

content.warning: Do not roll out chargeback before showback has run for at least 8 weeks and tag coverage is stable above 95%. Premature chargeback erodes trust and produces gaming behavior (engineers hide costs in shared accounts).
Never optimize commitments (RI/SP) above 90% coverage — you lose elasticity and end up paying for unused capacity. Sweet spot is 70-80%.
